title: lavandula pedunculata subsp. lusitanica
intro: Lavandula pedunculata subsp. lusitanica is a subspecies of the Lavandula pedunculata plant, a type of lavender native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and calming effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and anxiety. The health benefits of Lavandula pedunculata subsp. lusitanica include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, essential oils, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, calm the mind, and promote overall well-being.
